title: The evolution of pairing correlation with $3d_{z^{2}}$ electron filling in a bilayer two-orbital model for La$_3$Ni$_2$O$_7$
authors: Y. F. Chen, Y. Shen, X. J. Qian, G. M. Zhang, M. P. Qin
abstract: The discovery of high-${T_c}$ superconductivity in pressurized bilayer nickelate La$_3$Ni$_2$O$_7$ presents a new arena for exploring unconventional pairing mechanisms. A pivotal yet unresolved issue is the specific role of the $3d_{z^{2}}$ orbital of Ni. While its inter-layer super-exchange antiferromagnetic coupling is widely considered crucial for superconductivity, the role of its itinerancy remains undetermined. Early studies showed that the superconductivity is accompanied by the emergence of a small Fermi pocket of the $3d_{z^{2}}$ orbitals. However, recent experiments show controversial results on the role of the $3d_{z^{2}}$ Fermi pocket on superconductivity. Motivated by these experimental results, we investigate an effective bilayer two-orbital model for La$_3$Ni$_2$O$_7$ using density-matrix renormalization group (DMRG) on a minimal one-dimensional geometry. By systematically varying the $3d_{z^{2}}$ orbital filling from $1/12$ doping to half-filling, we observe a pronounced suppression of superconducting correlations near half-filling. Our results demonstrate the itinerancy of $3d_{z^{2}}$ orbital is favorable for the pairing in the bilayer two-orbital model for La$_3$Ni$_2$O$_7$. Moreover, we observe that the pairing correlation is enhanced in regions where charge fluctuations are large, suggesting a competition between charge order and superconductivity in the model.
summary: This paper investigates the mechanism of superconductivity in the bilayer nickelate La3Ni2O7 using DMRG simulations. It demonstrates that the itinerancy of the 3d_z2 orbital promotes pairing and that superconductivity is suppressed as this orbital approaches half-filling. The study also highlights a competition between charge density waves and superconductivity, with pairing enhanced near charge-fluctuation-rich singularity points.
main_problem: The study aims to resolve the role of the Ni 3d_z2 orbital in the high-Tc superconductivity of La3Ni2O7, specifically whether its itinerancy and the presence of its Fermi pocket are essential for pairing.
main_result: The researchers find that the itinerancy of the 3d_z2 orbital is favorable for pairing and that superconducting correlations are significantly suppressed as the 3d_z2 orbital approaches half-filling. They also identify that pairing is enhanced near specific singularity points characterized by large charge fluctuations.
method: Large-scale Density-Matrix Renormalization Group (DMRG) calculations on a one-dimensional cylindrical geometry with bond dimensions up to 18,000 and finite truncation error extrapolation.
model_or_system: An effective bilayer two-orbital model for the pressurized nickelate La3Ni2O7, incorporating Ni e_g orbitals (d_x2-y2 and d_z2) with inter-orbital hybridization, interlayer super-exchange coupling (J), and interlayer d_z2 hopping.
key_observables: Orbital-resolved charge and spin density distributions, pair-pair correlation functions for both orbitals, and the superconducting pairing exponent (K_SC) extracted from power-law decay.
important_parameters: Chemical potential difference (mu) used to tune 3d_z2 filling, interlayer super-exchange coupling (J = 0.5), and 3d_z2 orbital occupancy ranging from 1/12 doping to half-filling.
main_assumptions: The model uses a minimal one-dimensional geometry (chain/ladder) and treats the 3d_z2 orbital using a t-J-like approach where double occupancy is forbidden.
figures_summary: Figures 6 and 8 show charge density patterns and CDW wavelength changes at specific chemical potentials; Figures 7 and 9 display pairing correlation functions and their enhancement at singularities; Figures 10 and 11 illustrate the DMRG extrapolation and power-law fitting procedures.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of Ni 3d_z2 orbital role, describes the bilayer two-orbital model and DMRG methodology, presents results on charge density waves and orbital-specific pairing, analyzes the evolution of correlations with filling, and concludes with the competition between charge order and superconductivity.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Schematic representation of the one-dimensional setup studied in this work for the bilayer two-orbital model. The blue sites denote the dx2−y2 orbitals on the two outer legs, while the red sites denote the two dz2 orbitals in the central bilayer channel. The blue dashed rectangles indicate the nearest-neighbor hopping tx2−y2 along the dx2−y2 chains. The black dashed links represent the inter-orbital hybridiza- tion tx2−y2,z2, and the red dashed rectangles mark the inter- layer dz2 hopping and exchange channel, labeled tz2 and J.

FIG. 2. Orbital occupancy of the dz2 orbital as a function of the chemical potential difference µ. Larger µ continuously shifts the dz2 orbital toward half filling (⟨ndz2 ⟩= 1). The DMRG results with three bond dimensions nearly coincide, indicating that the orbital occupancy is well converged in the DMRG calculation. We notice that there exist two singularity points at µ ≈2.3 and 3.0.

FIG. 3. Charge and spin distributions of the dz2 orbital [(a),(c)] and the dx2−y2 orbital [(b),(d)] for representative values of ⟨ndz2 ⟩; the corresponding µ values are also shown in the legends. The DMRG results are with bond dimension up to D = 18000 with truncation error ϵ ∼10−6 and convergence with bond dimension is checked. Charge density waves with wave-length 4 and 5 are observed in both orbitals. Around the two singularity points in Fig. 2 with µ ≈2.3 and 3.0, the charge density wave pattern changes. See the main text for more discussion. Similar density wave pattern is observed in the spin channel but with weak amplitude.

FIG. 4. Pairing correlation functions of the dz2 and dx2−y2 orbitals for selected values of ⟨ndz2 ⟩; the corresponding µ values are also shown in the legends. The values are obtained by extrapolating finite-bond-dimension data to the zero-truncation-error limit ϵ →0. In both orbitals, the correlations exhibit power-law decay as a function of distance, while the long-distance pairing amplitude is progressively suppressed as ⟨ndz2 ⟩increases toward half filling. Around the two singularity points in Fig. 2, the pairing correlation is enhanced, consistent with the large charge fluctuation observed around these two points in Fig. 3.

FIG. 5. Power-law exponents KSC extracted from the pairing correlations of the dz2 and dx2−y2 orbitals as functions of ⟨ndz2 ⟩. The fits are performed using data extrapolated to the zero-truncation-error limit. Overall, KSC increases as the density in dz2 orbital is increased to half-filling for both the dz2 and dx2−y2 orbitals with dips found in the two singularity points in Fig. 2. A substantial increase of KSC occurs near ⟨ndz2 ⟩≈0.983 in both channels, indicating suppression of the superconducting tendency as the dz2 orbital approaches half filling.
